title: Aethel Server Database Blueprint
description: Configures PostgreSQL runtime for aethel-core (Go backend). See docs/server-blueprint-conventions.md §1.

version.description: SemVer. Bump minor on new fields, major on breaking changes.

engine_target.description: Minimum PostgreSQL version required by the migration set.

strict_validation.description: When true, unknown fields in this file cause a fatal startup error.

global_database_defaults.description: Defaults applied to all environments unless overridden.

dialect.description: Fixed to postgres. Do not change.

timezone.description: All timestamptz values are stored in this timezone.

log_queries.description: Log every SQL statement. Very high volume — disable in production.

slow_query_threshold_ms.description: Queries exceeding this duration (ms) are always logged.

environments.description: Per-environment connection, pooling, and migration config. Active env is selected by AETHEL_ENV.

host.description: Hostname or IP of the PostgreSQL server.

user.description: Database role/user. Never store passwords here — use AETHEL_DB_PASSWORD.

ssl_root_cert_path.description: Path to PEM CA certificate. Required when ssl_mode is verify-full.

connection_string_env.description: Name of an env var holding a full postgres:// DSN. Overrides all other connection fields when set.

pooling.description: Connection pool sizing. max_open_connections across all instances should not exceed PG max_connections minus 10.

max_open_connections.description: Maximum total connections to the PostgreSQL server.

max_idle_connections.description: Idle connections kept alive in the pool.

connection_max_idle_time_minutes.description: Close idle connections after N minutes of inactivity.

directory.description: Path to migration files, relative to the binary working directory.

auto_run_on_startup.description: Run pending migrations on startup. Never set to true in production.

table_name.description: Table used to track applied migrations.

lock_timeout_seconds.description: Advisory lock timeout — prevents two processes running migrations concurrently.

schema.description: Schema name and table/enum alias map. Aliases let IT rename tables without touching SQL files.

name_aliases.description: Map of canonical table names to deployed names. Omit a key to use the canonical name.

enum_aliases.description: Map of canonical PostgreSQL enum type names to deployed names.

partitioning.description: Range-partition strategies for large append-only tables.

type.description: Partition type. Only range is supported in v1.

interval.description: How frequently new partitions are created.

enabled.description: Enable automatic dropping of old partitions.

retain_months.description: Number of months of data to retain.

extensions.description: PostgreSQL extensions to install. Missing required extensions cause a fatal startup error.

required.description: Extensions that must be present. The migrator runs CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS for each.

optional.description: Extensions that improve performance but are not mandatory. Missing ones log a warning.

performance.description: Session-level PostgreSQL parameters set on each pool connection.

statement_timeout_ms.description: Hard limit on statement execution time (ms). 0 = disabled.

idle_in_transaction_timeout_ms.description: Kills idle transactions holding locks after this threshold (ms).

lock_timeout_ms.description: Aborts statements waiting on a lock for longer than this threshold (ms).
